nodes.description: Two-week intravenous ceftriaxone induction followed by oral TMP-SMX maintenance to eradicate infection and prevent relapse.

evidence.snippet: The German treatment recommendations include a two-week intravenous induction therapy with ceftriaxone, which is followed by a three-month oral maintenance therapy with trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole.

nodes.description: Oral doxycycline and hydroxychloroquine for one year, followed by lifelong doxycycline for relapse prevention.

evidence.snippet: A French group favors a combination of doxycycline and hydroxychloroquine for 1 year, followed by a lifelong administration of doxycycline.

nodes.description: Tropheryma whipplei, an actinobacterium, builds and maintains its peptidoglycan cell wall by penicillin-binding protein (PBP) transpeptidase cross-linking. This essential, bacteria-specific reaction is the target of the intravenous ceftriaxone induction used in Whipple disease; beta-lactam acylation of the PBP active site halts cross-linking and provides the bactericidal induction phase before TMP-SMX maintenance.

evidence.snippet: although glycan chain polymerization occurs in the absence of transpeptidation (for example, in the presence of penicillin), efficient transpeptidation requires ongoing GTase reactions

nodes.description: Tropheryma whipplei depends on 70S-ribosome translation. Doxycycline binds the 30S subunit and arrests bacterial protein synthesis; it anchors the alternative long-course oral regimen (with hydroxychloroquine) and lifelong relapse prophylaxis.

evidence.snippet: The ribosome is one of the main antibiotic targets in the bacterial cell.

nodes.description: Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (co-trimoxazole) — the standard oral maintenance therapy after ceftriaxone induction — inhibits the T. whipplei folate pathway at two enzymes: sulfamethoxazole blocks dihydropteroate synthase (DHPS) and trimethoprim blocks dihydrofolate reductase (DHFR), giving synergistic sequential blockade.

evidence.snippet: The other two enzyme families are involved in the synthesis of tetrahydrofolate (THF), i.e. dihydropteroate synthase (DHPS) and dihydrofolate reductase.
